1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
54			if err != nil {
55				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
56			}
57			svc = built
58		}
59		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
60	}
61	
62	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
63	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
64	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
65	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
66	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
67		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
68		if harness == "" {
69			harness = rcfg.Harness()
70		}
71		model := runtime.ModelOverride
72		if model == "" {
73			model = rcfg.Model()
74		}
75		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
76		if permissions == "" {
77			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
78		}
79		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
80		if sessionLogDir == "" {
81			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
82		}
83	
84		var opts RunArgs
85		opts.Context = ctx
86		opts.Harness = harness
87		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
88		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
89		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
90		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
91		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
92		opts.Model = model
93		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
94		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
95		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
96		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
97		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
98		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
99		opts.Permissions = permissions
100		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
101		return opts
102	}

84	// installStubService injects a service factory and registers cleanup.
85	func installStubService(t *testing.T, stub *stubAgentService) {
86		t.Helper()
87		agent.SetServiceRunFactory(func(_ string) (agentlib.DdxAgent, error) {
88			return stub, nil

93	// minimalProjectDir creates a project dir with a clean .ddx/config.yaml that
94	// has no agent.harness pin so flag values drive routing.
95	func minimalProjectDir(t *testing.T) string {
96		t.Helper()
97		dir := t.TempDir()
98		t.Setenv("HOME", dir)
99		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
100		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
101		cfg := `version: "1.0"
102	library:
103	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
104	  repository:
105	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
106	    branch: "main"
107	`
108		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))
109		return dir
110	}

112	// AC #1: ddx agent run --harness claude forwards Harness to Execute.
113	func TestRunHarnessFlagPlumbsToExecute(t *testing.T) {
114		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
115	
116		var capturedReq agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
117		installStubService(t, &stubAgentService{
118			execute: func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
119				capturedReq = req
120				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
121				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
122				close(ch)
123				return ch, nil
124			},
125		})
126	
127		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
128		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
129		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "run", "--harness", "claude", "--text", "hi", "--timeout", "5s")
130		require.NoError(t, err, "stub Execute returns success; if ResolveRoute were called, stub would error")
131		assert.Equal(t, "claude", capturedReq.Harness, "--harness must plumb through to ServiceExecuteRequest.Harness")
132	}

154	}

156	// AC #3: ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus forwards both to Execute.
157	func TestRunHarnessAndModelBothPlumbToExecute(t *testing.T) {
158		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
159	
160		var capturedReq agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
161		installStubService(t, &stubAgentService{
162			execute: func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
163				capturedReq = req

165				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}

171		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
172		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
173		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "run",
174			"--harness", "claude", "--model", "opus", "--text", "hi", "--timeout", "5s")
175		require.NoError(t, err, "stub Execute returns success; if ResolveRoute were called, stub would error")

177		assert.Equal(t, "opus", capturedReq.Model)

180	// AC #4: typed errors from Execute surface verbatim — DDx does not wrap them.
181	func TestRunTypedExecuteErrorSurfacesVerbatim(t *testing.T) {
182		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
183	
184		executeErr := fmt.Errorf("upstream: provider quota exhausted")
185		installStubService(t, &stubAgentService{
186			execute: func(_ agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
187				return nil, executeErr
188			},
189		})
190	
191		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
192		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
193		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "run", "--harness", "claude", "--text", "hi", "--timeout", "5s")
194		require.Error(t, err, "typed error from Execute must surface")
195		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "upstream: provider quota exhausted",
196			"execute error message must appear in returned error")
197	}
198	
199	// AC #5: ddx work shares the same passthrough plumbing — flags reach Execute.
200	func TestWorkSharesSamePassthroughPlumbing(t *testing.T) {
201		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
202	
203		var capturedReq agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
204		installStubService(t, &stubAgentService{
205			execute: func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
206				capturedReq = req
207				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
208				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
209				close(ch)
210				return ch, nil
211			},
212		})
213	
214		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
215	
216		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
217		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
218		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
219			ID:    "ddx-work-passthrough-test",
220			Title: "work passthrough fixture",
221		}))
222	
223		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
224		_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work",
225			"--local", "--once",
226			"--harness", "claude",
227			"--model", "gpt-5.4",
228		)
229	
230		if capturedReq.Harness == "" && capturedReq.Model == "" {
231			t.Skip("Execute not called — bead may not have reached dispatch")
232		}
233	
234		assert.Equal(t, "claude", capturedReq.Harness,
235			"ddx work must plumb --harness to Execute.Harness")
236		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5.4", capturedReq.Model,
237			"ddx work must plumb --model to Execute.Model")
238	}

25	type ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct {
26		// ProjectRoot overrides the manager's default project root for this worker.
27		// When set, the worker scans and executes beads from this project instead of
28		// the server's primary working directory. Must be an absolute path to a
29		// directory containing a .ddx/ folder. Validated by the server before the
30		// worker starts.
31		ProjectRoot  string        `json:"project_root,omitempty"`
32		Harness      string        `json:"harness,omitempty"`

42		NoReview      bool   `json:"no_review,omitempty"`

45	}

55	
56	// Terminal phases per FEAT-006.
57	var terminalPhases = map[string]bool{
58		"done":      true,

60		"failed":    true,

62	
63	// CurrentAttemptInfo is the in-flight attempt summary embedded in WorkerRecord.
64	type CurrentAttemptInfo struct {

104	type WorkerRecord struct {
105		ID             string                 `json:"id"`
106		Kind           string                 `json:"kind"`
107		State          string                 `json:"state"`
108		Status         string                 `json:"status,omitempty"`

111		Provider       string                 `json:"provider,omitempty"`

116		PollInterval   string                 `json:"poll_interval,omitempty"`
117		StartedAt      time.Time              `json:"started_at,omitempty"`
118		FinishedAt     time.Time              `json:"finished_at,omitempty"`
119		Error          string                 `json:"error,omitempty"`

124		Failures       int                    `json:"failures,omitempty"`
125		CurrentBead    string                 `json:"current_bead,omitempty"`

128		CurrentAttempt *CurrentAttemptInfo    `json:"current_attempt,omitempty"`

132		LandSummary    *CoordinatorMetrics    `json:"land_summary,omitempty"`
133		// PID is the OS process id of an external worker subprocess, if any.
134		// Zero for purely in-process (goroutine-only) workers. Surfaced so the
135		// autonomous watchdog can send SIGTERM/SIGKILL to the process group when
136		// cancelling the context is not enough.
137		PID int `json:"pid,omitempty"`
138		// ReapReason is populated when the watchdog forcibly terminates a worker;
139		// set to "watchdog" today.
140		ReapReason string `json:"reap_reason,omitempty"`
141		// PIDAlive is a computed field populated by List(); it is never persisted
142		// to disk. True when PID > 0 and the process is alive, false when PID > 0
143		// but the process has exited. Omitted (nil) when PID == 0 (goroutine-only).
144		PIDAlive *bool `json:"pid_alive,omitempty"`
145	}
146	
147	type WorkerExecutionResult struct {
148		BeadID     string `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
149		AttemptID  string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
150		WorkerID   string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
151		Harness    string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
152		Provider   string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
153		Model      string `json:"model,omitempty"`
154		Status     string `json:"status,omitempty"`
155		Detail     string `json:"detail,omitempty"`

158		ResultRev  string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
159		RetryAfter string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
160	}
161	
162	type workerHandle struct {
163		record  WorkerRecord
164		cancel  context.CancelFunc
165		logBuf  *bytes.Buffer
166		logFile *os.File
167		// progressCh receives ProgressEvents from the execute-bead loop.
168		// The WorkerManager drains this channel to update WorkerRecord and
169		// broadcast to SSE subscribers.
170		progressCh chan agent.ProgressEvent
171		// progressSubs holds active SSE subscriber channels for this worker.
172		progressSubs []chan agent.ProgressEvent
173		// progressDone is closed when drainProgress exits, signalling that
174		// no further events will arrive and all new subscriptions should
175		// receive an immediately-closed channel.
176		progressDone chan struct{}
177		// lastPhaseTS is the wall-clock time of the most recent non-heartbeat
178		// ProgressEvent. The watchdog uses this to detect stalled attempts.
179		lastPhaseTS time.Time
180		// reaped is set true once the watchdog has escalated this worker. It is
181		// checked under m.mu to make reaping idempotent.
182		reaped bool
183		// stopped is set true once an operator-driven Stop has started the
184		// graceful termination path. Checked under m.mu so a second Stop is a
185		// no-op and runWorker can preserve the "stopped" state across its final
186		// record write.
187		stopped bool
188	}
189	
190	// WorkerManager manages in-process execute-loop workers as goroutines.
191	type WorkerManager struct {
192		projectRoot string
193		rootDir     string
194		// BeadWorkerFactory, when non-nil, is called by runWorker to create the
195		// ExecuteBeadWorker instead of building one from the real agent runner.
196		// Override in tests to inject a fake executor.
197		BeadWorkerFactory func(store agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore) *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker
198	
199		// LandCoordinators is the per-project registry of land coordinators.
200		// Exported so tests and server integration tests can stop coordinators
201		// on teardown, or inject a custom LandingGitOps via
202		// LandCoordinators.gitOpsOverride.
203		LandCoordinators *coordinatorRegistry
204	
205		// Watchdog parameters. Zero values fall back to defaults:
206		//   WatchdogDeadline      = 6h  (total worker runtime budget)
207		//   StallDeadline         = 1h  (max phase-transition gap before reap)
208		//   WatchdogCheckInterval = 1m  (how often the supervisor sweeps)
209		//   WatchdogKillGrace     = 30s (SIGTERM → SIGKILL grace window)
210		// Tests override these to run the watchdog on millisecond scales.
211		WatchdogDeadline      time.Duration
212		StallDeadline         time.Duration
213		WatchdogCheckInterval time.Duration
214		WatchdogKillGrace     time.Duration
215	
216		mu      sync.Mutex
217		workers map[string]*workerHandle
218	
219		watchdogOnce sync.Once
220		watchdogStop chan struct{}
221	}
222	
223	const (
224		defaultWatchdogDeadline      = 6 * time.Hour
225		defaultStallDeadline         = 1 * time.Hour
226		defaultWatchdogCheckInterval = 1 * time.Minute
227		defaultWatchdogKillGrace     = 30 * time.Second
228	)
229	
230	func NewWorkerManager(projectRoot string) *WorkerManager {
231		m := &WorkerManager{
232			projectRoot:      projectRoot,
233			rootDir:          filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers"),
234			workers:          map[string]*workerHandle{},
235			LandCoordinators: newCoordinatorRegistry(),
236			watchdogStop:     make(chan struct{}),
237		}
238		m.applyServerWatchdogConfig(projectRoot)
239		return m
240	}
241	
242	func lifecycleStartDetail(spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec) string {
243		parts := []string{"kind=execute-loop"}
244		if spec.Harness != "" {
245			parts = append(parts, "harness="+spec.Harness)
246		}
247		if spec.Profile != "" {
248			parts = append(parts, "profile="+agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile))
249		}
250		if spec.Effort != "" {
251			parts = append(parts, "effort="+spec.Effort)
252		}
253		if spec.LabelFilter != "" {
254			parts = append(parts, "label_filter="+spec.LabelFilter)
255		}
256		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
257	}
258	
259	// applyServerWatchdogConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and applies
260	// any server.watchdog_deadline / server.stall_deadline overrides. Invalid or
261	// missing values are silently ignored — defaults are filled in by the
262	// watchdog loop at runtime.
263	func (m *WorkerManager) applyServerWatchdogConfig(projectRoot string) {
264		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
265		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Server == nil {
266			return
267		}
268		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.WatchdogDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
269			m.WatchdogDeadline = d
270		}
271		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(cfg.Server.StallDeadline); err == nil && d > 0 {
272			m.StallDeadline = d
273		}
274	}
275	
276	// watchdogDeadlines returns the effective deadlines, applying defaults for
277	// any zero-valued fields.
278	func (m *WorkerManager) watchdogDeadlines() (watchdog, stall, check, grace time.Duration) {
279		watchdog = m.WatchdogDeadline
280		if watchdog <= 0 {
281			watchdog = defaultWatchdogDeadline
282		}
283		stall = m.StallDeadline
284		if stall <= 0 {
285			stall = defaultStallDeadline
286		}
287		check = m.WatchdogCheckInterval
288		if check <= 0 {

45	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
46	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
47	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
48	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
49	//
50	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
51	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
52	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
53	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
54	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
55	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
56	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
57	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
58		if ctx == nil {
59			ctx = context.Background()
60		}
61	
62		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
63	
64		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
65			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
66			if sessionLogDir == "" {
67				sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
68			}
69			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")}
70			r := NewRunner(cfg)
71			r.WorkDir = workDir
72			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{
73				Context:       ctx,
74				Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
75				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,
76				PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
77				PromptSource:  runtime.PromptSource,
78				Correlation:   runtime.Correlation,
79				Model:         rcfg.Model(),
80				Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
81				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
82				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
83				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
84				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
85				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
86				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
87				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
88			})
89		}
90	
91		factory := serviceRunFactory
92		if factory == nil {
93			factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
94		}
95		svc, err := factory(workDir)
96		if err != nil {
97			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
98		}
99		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, workDir, rcfg, runtime)
100	}

106	func executeOnService(ctx context.Context, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {

634		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
635			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
636		}
637		defer func() {
638			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)
639		}()
640	
641		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
642		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
643		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
644		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
645		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
646			BeadID:       beadID,
647			AttemptID:    attemptID,
648			Harness:      rcfg.Harness(),
649			Model:        rcfg.Model(),
650			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
651			WorktreePath: wtPath,
652		})
653		defer func() {
654			_ = ClearRunState(projectRoot)
655		}()
656	
657		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
658		// the freshly created worktree.
659		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
660	
661		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
662		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, rcfg, runtime)
663		if err != nil {
664			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
665				BeadID:    beadID,
666				AttemptID: attemptID,
667				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
668				BaseRev:   baseRev,
669				ResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
670				ExitCode:  1,
671				Error:     err.Error(),
672				Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
673			}
674			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
675				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
676			}
677			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
678			populateWorkerStatus(res)
679			_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID, "result.json"), res)
680			return res, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead context load: %w", err)
681		}
682	
683		// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write
684		// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the
685		// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.
686		_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)
687	
688		// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution
689		// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.
690		embeddedStateDir := filepath.Join(artifacts.DirAbs, "embedded")
691		if err := os.MkdirAll(embeddedStateDir, 0o755); err != nil {
692			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating embedded state dir: %w", err)
693		}
694	
695		sessionID := GenerateSessionID()
696		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
697

697	
698		runRuntime := AgentRunRuntime{
699			PromptFile: artifacts.PromptAbs,
700			WorkDir:    wtPath,
701			Correlation: map[string]string{
702				"bead_id":     beadID,
703				"base_rev":    baseRev,
704				"attempt_id":  attemptID,
705				"session_id":  sessionID,
706				"worker_id":   runtime.WorkerID,
707				"bundle_path": artifacts.DirRel,
708				"prompt_file": artifacts.PromptRel,
709			},
710			SessionLogDirOverride: embeddedStateDir,
711			PermissionsOverride:   "unrestricted", // isolated worktree; writes must not require approval
712		}
713	
714		agentResult, agentErr := dispatchAgentRun(ctx, projectRoot, runtime.Service, runtime.AgentRunner, rcfg, runRuntime)
715		finishedAt := time.Now().UTC()
716	
717		exitCode := 0
718		tokens := 0
719		inputTokens := 0
720		outputTokens := 0
721		costUSD := 0.0
722		resultModel := rcfg.Model()
723		resultHarness := rcfg.Harness()
724		resultProvider := ""
725		actualPower := 0
726		agentErrMsg := ""
727		if agentResult != nil {
728			exitCode = agentResult.ExitCode
729			tokens = agentResult.Tokens
730			inputTokens = agentResult.InputTokens
731			outputTokens = agentResult.OutputTokens
732			costUSD = agentResult.CostUSD
733			if agentResult.Error != "" {
734				agentErrMsg = agentResult.Error
735			}
736			if agentResult.Model != "" {
737				resultModel = agentResult.Model
738			}
739			if agentResult.Provider != "" {
740				resultProvider = agentResult.Provider
741			}
742			if agentResult.Harness != "" {
743				resultHarness = agentResult.Harness
744			}
745			if agentResult.ActualPower > 0 {
746				actualPower = agentResult.ActualPower
747			}
748		}
749		if agentErr != nil {
750			if exitCode == 0 {
751				exitCode = 1
752			}
753			agentErrMsg = agentErr.Error()
754		}
755	
756		// Capture routing evidence from the agent result. These fields are
757		// populated by RunAgent (embedded harness) and RunScript (script harness).
758		routeReason := ""
759		routeBaseURL := ""
760		if agentResult != nil {
761			routeReason = agentResult.RouteReason
762			routeBaseURL = agentResult.ResolvedBaseURL
763		}
764	
765		// Get the HEAD of the worktree after the agent ran.
766		resultRev, revErr := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath)
767		if revErr != nil {
768			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
769				BeadID:       beadID,
770				AttemptID:    attemptID,
771				WorkerID:     runtime.WorkerID,
772				BaseRev:      baseRev,
773				ResultRev:    baseRev, // no commits readable; treat as no output
774				Harness:      resultHarness,
775				Provider:     resultProvider,
776				Model:        resultModel,

git add cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go cli/cmd/agent_run_overrides_e2e_test.go cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go cli/internal/config/resolved.go cli/internal/config/resolved_test.go cli/internal/server/workers.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(routing): ddx work path uses power-only Execute; no DDx-side model injection [ddx-d06ae28c]

Implements CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 (ddx-c4231775): the ddx work default path
must not inject DDx-selected harness or model into Execute calls. The agent
service owns routing; DDx only passes MinPower/MaxPower as abstract bounds.

  and RoutePreflight (svc.ResolveRoute) when true — ddx work must not call
  ResolveRoute before dispatch
- Fix dispatchViaResolvedConfig to use serviceRunFactory instead of calling
  NewServiceFromWorkDir directly, enabling cmd-level stub injection for
  execute-bead dispatch tests
- Add TestOpaquePassthroughBlocksConfigHarnessModel (config unit test) and
  TestWorkCleanConfigNoModelInjected (regression guard for the historical
  axon/openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini silent mini-model injection bug)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Implementation complete. Here's a summary of what was changed across 6 files:

**`cli/internal/config/resolved.go`** — Added `OpaquePassthrough bool` to `CLIOverrides`; `Resolve()` now gates the `agent.harness`/`agent.model` config fallback behind `!overrides.OpaquePassthrough`.

**`cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`** — Fixed `dispatchViaResolvedConfig` to use `serviceRunFactory` (the test injection hook) instead of calling `NewServiceFromWorkDir` directly, enabling cmd-level stub injection for execute-bead dispatch tests.

**`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`** — Wired `OpaquePassthrough: treatPassthroughAsOpaque` into `singleTierAttempt`'s `CLIOverrides`; added `opaquePassthrough bool` param to `executeLoopWithServer` and passes `opaque_passthrough: true` in the worker spec JSON when set.

**`cli/internal/server/workers.go`** — Added `OpaquePassthrough bool` to `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec`; skips `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` and the `RoutePreflight` (which calls `svc.ResolveRoute`) when `OpaquePassthrough=true`; propagates the flag through `singleTierAttempt`'s `CLIOverrides`.

**`cli/internal/config/resolved_test.go`** — Added `TestOpaquePassthroughBlocksConfigHarnessModel`: unit test verifying config injection is blocked with `OpaquePassthrough=true` while explicit CLI values still pass through.

**`cli/cmd/agent_run_overrides_e2e_test.go`** — Added `TestWorkCleanConfigNoModelInjected`: regression guard for the axon/gpt-5.4-mini silent injection bug — asserts that `ddx work` with a config pinning `agent.model` does not inject that value into `ServiceExecuteRequest`.
